generated_parts.label: Integration Harness + Datum Spine
generated_parts.spec: Integration_harness closure spine with harness_channel, connector_keying, assembly_datum pads, routing clips, service hatch, reciprocal interface labels, inspection checklist marks

verify.value: Battery, sensor modules remain removable without full teardown.
Harnesses are routed through visible raceways.
Access is represented by a service hatch.
Strain relief geometry and bend radius are respected at cable exits.
Connectors are reachable, have clearance, and use keying.
Assembly datum pads are visible and labeled.
Critical interfaces state plausible clearances.
Subsystems has 3 adjacent interfaces.
Fits use standard intent with clearance fit for sliding.
Holes match catalog hardware sizes (M3 clearance holes).
Material/process is explicit.
Verification items reference real features.
Each requirement maps to a verification method.
Cross-subsystem interfaces state mating geometry and exchanged data.
Interfaces name connection type (bolted, connector_mate) with DOF and clearance.
Parts can be installed in a stated order.
Parts locate off a named parent datum using 3-2-1 scheme.
Every part's load traces to the base.
Design moves in the right direction: fewer interfaces, open load paths closed.
Assembly uses fewest interfaces necessary.

risk.desc: Connector strain relief may not be sufficient for vibration loads.
risk.mit: Use a more robust strain relief mechanism or add potting.

risk.desc: Sharp edges near harness routing could damage wires.
risk.mit: Add edge fillets or protective sleeving.

physics_paths: harness_channel: electrical data paths
connector_keying: mechanical load path
assembly_datum: structural alignment

manufacturing_strategy.datum: primary datum face on base plate
